heading: Consumer privacy and unfair trade practices
label: 16471 Consumer privacy and unfair trade practices

children.heading: Privacy
children.label: a Privacy
children.text: The Federal Trade Commission may issue rules protecting the privacy of electric consumers from the disclosure of consumer information obtained in connection with the sale or delivery of electric energy to electric consumers.

children.heading: Slamming
children.label: b Slamming
children.text: The Federal Trade Commission may issue rules prohibiting the change of selection of an electric utility except with the informed consent of the electric consumer or if approved by the appropriate State regulatory authority.

children.heading: Cramming
children.label: c Cramming
children.text: The Federal Trade Commission may issue rules prohibiting the sale of goods and services to an electric consumer unless expressly authorized by law or the electric consumer.

children.heading: Rulemaking
children.label: d Rulemaking
children.text: The Federal Trade Commission shall proceed in accordance with when prescribing a rule under this section.section 553 of title 5

children.heading: State authority
children.label: e State authority
children.text: If the Federal Trade Commission determines that a State’s regulations provide equivalent or greater protection than the provisions of this section, such State regulations shall apply in that State in lieu of the regulations issued by the Commission under this section.

children.heading: State regulatory authority
children.label: 1 State regulatory authority
children.text: The term “State regulatory authority” has the meaning given that term in .section 796(21) of title 16

children.heading: Electric consumer and electric utility
children.label: 2 Electric consumer and electric utility
children.text: The terms “electric consumer” and “electric utility” have the meanings given those terms in .section 2602 of title 16
